description: A flat-illustration winter-safety infographic on a light blue background. The top half shows a stylized cityscape/skyline of blue and gray buildings topped with snow, a snow-capped lamppost, and a snow-covered house with an evergreen tree, behind a pale panel holding the title 'WINTER WEATHER wisdom' (WINTER WEATHER in bold dark navy caps, 'wisdom' in lighter script-like lowercase). The bottom half is a 2x2 grid of safety tips separated by dashed lines: 'Keep heaters at least 3 feet from anything that can catch fire.'; 'Check on your neighbors to make sure they're okay.'; 'Only use generators outside, at least 20 feet from windows & doors.'; 'Never use your oven to heat your home - it can be a fire hazard.' Bottom-right shows the 'Ready' logo (with a green check) and the DHS seal with 'FEMA' wordmark. This is a FEMA/Ready.gov seasonal preparedness PSA, not immigration content. Friendly flat-vector style. Target audience: general public, winter safety.
notable_text: WINTER WEATHER wisdom; Keep heaters at least 3 feet from anything that can catch fire; Only use generators outside, at least 20 feet from windows & doors; Never use your oven to heat your home
